title: POST for Grid List Endpoints
context: Grid/list endpoints need to accept complex filter payloads: pagination (page, pageSize), sorting, search text, multi-select enum filters (EnumDTO arrays), date ranges, and nested filter objects. These payloads are awkward in query strings, especially with EnumDTO objects.
decision: All grid-list endpoints use **POST** with the grid request payload in the request body.
This route is the documented API surface for server-side paging, sorting, filtering, and search from `nxgn-data-grid` and related grid clients.
Compatibility aliases may be registered only to preserve existing clients or known UI experiments:
Aliases are additive. They must call the same handler as `grid-list`, and frontend constants, PRDs, designs, tests, and new documentation should point to `grid-list`.
Do not introduce new `/list` grid routes. Lookup/dropdown endpoints remain separate and may use `GET` when query parameters are sufficient.
consequences: - Complex grid requests can carry filters, sort descriptors, search text, paging, and future metadata without URL length limits.
- `grid-list` names the route by its UI/API contract instead of implying a general list or lookup endpoint.
- Compatibility aliases allow older UI code to keep working while docs and new modules converge.
- POST grid-list routes require clients and tests to avoid assuming all reads are GET requests.
- Route aliases can hide drift if teams document or test the alias instead of the canonical `grid-list` route.
- Treat `grid-list` as the only canonical route in generated clients, frontend route constants, browser plans, and API docs.
- Keep aliases in endpoint registration only when there is an explicit compatibility reason.
- Integration tests should cover the canonical route; alias tests should be narrow compatibility tests.
origin: Capstone convention, adopted by Identity (ADR-0020), adopted by Actions for filter complexity.
